ocr.text: In the context of MetLife's ongoing analysis of actuarial reserve movements, the data presented in the accompanying table
provides critical insights into the company's financial health and risk management strategies across various categories of
insurance. The evaluation of reserves is essential for ensuring that MetLife maintains adequate financial backing for future
claims.
ActuarialReserveMovements  FY2023  EUR
Category  OpeningReserve  ClosingReserve  Change  ReserveRatio  ClaimsRatio  ExpenseRatio  FundingRatio
LifeInsurance  500M  520M  20M  1.04  0.85  [HIDDEN]  1.10
HealthInsurance  300M  290M  -10M  0.97  0.75  0.15  1.05
PropertyInsurance  200M  210M  10M  1.05  0.90  0.25  1.15
LiabilityInsurance  [HIDDEN]  160M  10M  1.07  [HIDDEN]  0.18  1.08
InvestmentIncome  40M  38M  -2M  0.95  0.70  0.10  1.00
TotalRevenue  1190M  1218M  28M  1.02  [HIDDEN]  0.20  1.11
ReinsuranceRecoverables  30M  [HIDDEN]  -2M  0.93  0.72  0.12  0.95
NetReserves  1120M  1150M  30M  1.03  0.80  [HIDDEN]  1.10
RegulatoryCapital  500M  510M  10M  1.02  0.78  0.15  1.05
Notably, the "LiabilityInsurance" category reflects an "OpeningReserve" of EUR 150M, which underscores the significant
obligations the company must manage. Furthermore, the "ClaimsRatio" for this category stands at a substantial 0.80, indicating
that a significant portion of the reserves is dedicated to covering claims. This is complemented by insights from the
"TotalRevenue" category, where the "ClaimsRatio" also mirrors this figure at 0.80, reinforcing the correlation between revenue
and claims liabilities. Additionally, the "ReinsuranceRecoverables" category shows a robust "ClosingReserve" of EUR 28M,
highlighting the recoverable amounts anticipated from reinsurers, which play a vital role in mitigating risks associated with
large claims. Lastly, in terms of operational efficiency, the "LifeInsurance" line reflects an "ExpenseRatio" of 0.20, which is
consistent with the "NetReserves" category, also showing an "ExpenseRatio" of 0.20. This alignment suggests a strategic focus
on maintaining a balanced approach to expense management while ensuring sufficient reserves to meet policyholder
obligations. Overall, these metrics collectively illustrate MetLife’s proactive stance in managing its actuarial reserves amidst a
dynamic insurance landscape.
